response:
# Perspective Audit — Login Form (CLEAN) ## Step 1 — Confirmed Escalated Perspectives Per the a11y-critic escalation: - **Cognitive & Neurodivergent — MEDIUM** → deep review (this audit) All other perspectives were rated LOW or not flagged and are **not** reviewed here (evidence over assertion — the checklist only fires where the risk signal exists). **Component under review:** `LoginForm` (React + CSS), single-step email/password sign-in form with client-side validation, error summary, inline field errors, remember-me checkbox, and forgot-password / create-account links. --- ## Step 4 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent Checklist **WCAG criteria (AA):** 1.3.5, 2.2.1, 2.4.5, 3.2.1, 3.2.2, 3.2.3, 3.2.4, 3.2.6, 3.3.3, 3.3.4, 3.3.7, 3.3.8 **Aspirational AAA:** 3.1.3, 3.1.4, 3.1.5, 3.3.6 ### JTBD items | # | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Error messages suggest specific corrective action, not just "invalid input" (3.3.3) | **PASS** | `validate()` returns actionable text: `'Email address is required.'`, `'Please enter a valid email address.'`, `'Password is required.'`, `'Password must be at least 8 characters.'`. The password rule states the exact requirement ("at least 8 characters"), telling the user what to do, not just that it failed. | | 2 | Multi-step forms do not re-ask earlier information (3.3.7 Redundant Entry) | **N/A** | Single-step form; no prior step to re-ask from. No redundant entry possible. | | 3 | Authentication does not require transcription/memorization/puzzle-solving; paste and autofill work (3.3.8 Accessible Authentication) | **PASS** | Standard `` with no `onPaste`/`preventDefault` — paste is not blocked. `autoComplete="email"` and `autoComplete="current-password"` enable password-manager autofill. No CAPTCHA or cognitive-function test present. This is the strongest cognitive-load safeguard in the form. | | 4 | Navigation in same relative position across pages (3.2.3 Consistent Navigation) | **N/A (scope)** | Cross-page criterion; the fixture is a single component with no persistent site navigation. Cannot be assessed from this source — requires multi-page review. | | 5 | Same-function components have consistent labels/icons across pages (3.2.4 Consistent Identification) | **N/A (scope)** | Cross-page criterion; not assessable from a single component. | | 6 | Help mechanism in same location on every page (3.2.6 Consistent Help) | **N/A (scope)** | Self-help options ("Forgot password?", "Create account") are present at the end of the form. Cross-page consistency cannot be verified from a single component — requires multi-page review. Presence is a positive signal. | | 7 | Focus on element does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1 On Focus) | **PASS** | No `onFocus` handlers anywhere; focusing any control changes nothing. | | 8 | Changing a form control value does not auto-submit/auto-navigate (3.2.2 On Input) | **PASS** | `onChange` on email/password only updates state and clears that field's error; the checkbox `onChange` only sets `rememberMe`. Submission happens exclusively via the submit button / Enter, never on input change. | | 9 | Destructive actions have undo/confirmation/review (3.3.4 Error Prevention — Legal/Financial/Data) | **N/A** | Sign-in is not a legal, financial, or data-deletion transaction; no destructive action exists. | | 10 | More than one way to reach any page (2.4.5 Multiple Ways) | **N/A (scope)** | Site-level criterion; not assessable from a single component. The form does surface alternate paths (forgot-password, register). | | 11 | Personal-data inputs have appropriate `autocomplete` (1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose) | **PASS** | `autoComplete="email"` on the email field and `autoComplete="current-password"` on the password field — both are correct WCAG/HTML autofill tokens. Remember-me checkbox carries no personal data and needs none. | | 12 | Timeout warnings give ≥20s to extend (2.2.1 Timing Adjustable) | **N/A** | No timeout, session limit, or time-based interaction in the component. | | 13 | Content avoids unexplained jargon, undefined abbreviations, complex sentences | **PASS** | All visible copy is plain, common language: "Sign In", "Email address", "Password", "Remember me", "Forgot password?", "Create account", and the error strings. No jargon or abbreviations to define. | ### Neurodivergent-specific checks | Item | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---| | Page layout is predictable — main content in expected location, no unexpected layout shifts | **PASS** | Single-column form inside a `
` landmark. The only layout changes (error summary appearing above the form, inline `field-error` paragraphs appearing under inputs) are **user-initiated** (triggered by pressing Sign In) and predictable — not spontaneous reflow. | | Visual hierarchy is clear — headings, spacing, grouping guide reading order | **PASS** | `

Sign In

` for the page, `

` scoping the error summary, `.form-field` grouping with block labels and consistent `margin-bottom` spacing. Reading order follows a clear top-to-bottom flow. | | No autoplay media or unprompted modal dialogs | **PASS** | No media and no modals in the component. | | Instructions don't rely on memory of previous steps | **PASS** | Single-step form; persistent visible `

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
